text.title: Video Async API — Failure Modes
text.summary: Five antipatterns in video gen async clients.

failure-mode.description: Tight polling — sleep 100ms in poll loop; hits 429; provider rate-limits the account.
failure-mode.detector: poll loop sleeps <1s OR no backoff implemented
failure-mode.repair: Implement exponential backoff (1, 2, 4, 8, 16, 30). Add jitter to spread retries.

failure-mode.description: No idempotency key — network retry double-bills.
failure-mode.detector: submit() calls don't include idempotency-key header
failure-mode.repair: Compute sha256(prompt + params + provider) as key. Provider dedups on the key.

failure-mode.description: No fallback — Runway has 4-hour outage; media pipeline stalls; content backlog grows.
failure-mode.repair: Add fallback (e.g., Runway primary, Luma fallback). On timeout/failure, retry with fallback.

failure-mode.description: Missed artefact TTL — URL expired in 48h; downstream pipeline references dead link.
failure-mode.detector: downstream stores provider URL as final artefact reference
failure-mode.repair: Download immediately on SUCCEEDED. Store in S3/GCS. Reference internal URL only.

failure-mode.description: No cost cap — runaway batch generates 1000 videos at $5 each; $5000 bill.
failure-mode.detector: no monthly cost_budget enforced in submit()
failure-mode.repair: Check current spend before submit. If above cap, queue or refuse. Alert at 80%.
